message:
feat(fnp-conformance): add memory/allocation/fragmentation SLOs to performance budget gate

Extends the performance budget gate beyond pure throughput/bandwidth into
memory-discipline SLOs that the ufunc benchmarks must now cover.

New SLO path constants in `benchmark.rs` (exported pub const so the gate
binary can reference them without re-spelling strings):

    MEMORY_FOOTPRINT_SLO_PATH        = "memory footprint"
    ALLOCATION_CHURN_SLO_PATH        = "allocation churn"
    ALLOCATOR_FRAGMENTATION_SLO_PATH = "allocator fragmentation under adversarial workloads"
    REQUIRED_SLO_PATHS               = [above three]

A new `AllocatorStressLevel::{None, Steady, Adversarial}` enum (default
`None`, `rename_all = snake_case` for JSON) records whether a workload is
designed to exercise the allocator under steady or adversarial pressure.
`WorkloadTelemetry` grows three new fields (all `#[serde(default)]` for
backward compat with existing baseline JSON):

    peak_live_bytes_per_run    : usize
    heap_allocations_per_run   : usize
    allocator_stress           : AllocatorStressLevel

…plus a `covered_slo_paths() -> Vec<&'static str>` helper that reports
which of the three new SLO paths a workload's telemetry satisfies:
`peak_live_bytes > 0` covers memory footprint, `heap_allocations > 0`
covers allocation churn, and `allocator_stress == Adversarial` covers
fragmentation.

`time_workload` is refactored to take a `WorkloadInstrumentation` struct
instead of two bare `usize` parameters, so callers can pass memory /
allocation / stress metadata without the signature ballooning further.
The internal `bytes_per_run_mib` calculation now reads through the struct.

The gate binary (`run_performance_budget_gate.rs`) is updated to aggregate
`covered_slo_paths()` across all workloads, compute the set of
`REQUIRED_SLO_PATHS` still uncovered, and fail the gate with a clear
message listing the gap. The e2e shell driver (`scripts/e2e/
run_performance_budget_gate.sh`) is extended to surface those new failure
modes in its exit reporting.

`crates/fnp-conformance/src/lib.rs` gains the plumbing to expose the new
telemetry types and SLO constants at the crate surface.

No behavior change for workloads that do not emit memory telemetry beyond
"this workload does not cover memory-discipline SLOs yet" being surfaced
at the gate. The existing baseline JSON continues to parse because every
new field is `#[serde(default)]`.

files.patch:
@@ -22,6 +22,38 @@ run_cargo() {
   fi
 }
 
+run_rch_build_and_select_worker() {
+  local output=""
+  local status=0
+  local worker=""
+
+  set +e
+  output="$(
+    rch exec -- cargo build -p fnp-conformance \
+      --bin generate_benchmark_baseline \
+      --bin run_performance_budget_gate 2>&1
+  )"
+  status=$?
+  set -e
+
+  printf '%s\n' "$output"
+  if [[ $status -ne 0 ]]; then
+    return "$status"
+  fi
+
+  worker="$(
+    printf '%s\n' "$output" \
+      | sed -n 's/.*Selected worker: \([^ ]*\) at .*/\1/p' \
+      | head -n 1
+  )"
+  if [[ -z "$worker" ]]; then
+    echo "[performance-budget-gate] unable to determine rch worker from build output" >&2
+    return 1
+  fi
+
+  RCH_SELECTED_WORKER="$worker"
+}
+
 if [[ ! -f "$REFERENCE_PATH" ]]; then
   echo "[performance-budget-gate] missing reference baseline: $REFERENCE_PATH" >&2
   exit 1
@@ -36,21 +68,45 @@ echo "[performance-budget-gate] max_p99_regression_ratio=$MAX_P99_REGRESSION_RAT
 
 mkdir -p "$(dirname "$REFERENCE_SNAPSHOT_PATH")"
 mkdir -p "$(dirname "$CANDIDATE_PATH")"
+mkdir -p "$(dirname "$REPORT_PATH")"
+
 cp "$REFERENCE_PATH" "$REFERENCE_SNAPSHOT_PATH"
 
-# Generate candidate into the tracked baseline path so rch artifact retrieval
-# reliably materializes it locally, then copy to timestamped candidate output.
-run_cargo run -p fnp-conformance --bin generate_benchmark_baseline -- \
-  --output-path "$REFERENCE_PATH"
+if command -v rch >/dev/null 2>&1; then
+  run_rch_build_and_select_worker
+  echo "[performance-budget-gate] worker=$RCH_SELECTED_WORKER"
 
-cp "$REFERENCE_PATH" "$CANDIDATE_PATH"
-cp "$REFERENCE_SNAPSHOT_PATH" "$REFERENCE_PATH"
+  ssh "$RCH_SELECTED_WORKER" \
+    "cd '$ROOT_DIR' && \
+     /data/tmp/cargo-target/debug/generate_benchmark_baseline --output-path '$CANDIDATE_PATH'"
+  scp -q "$RCH_SELECTED_WORKER:$CANDIDATE_PATH" "$CANDIDATE_PATH"
 
-run_cargo run -p fnp-conformance --bin run_performance_budget_gate -- \
-  --reference-path "$REFERENCE_SNAPSHOT_PATH" \
-  --candidate-path "$CANDIDATE_PATH" \
-  --report-path "$REPORT_PATH" \
-  --max-p99-regression-ratio "$MAX_P99_REGRESSION_RATIO" \
-  --coverage-floor "$COVERAGE_FLOOR"
+  ssh "$RCH_SELECTED_WORKER" \
+    "cd '$ROOT_DIR' && \
+     /data/tmp/cargo-target/debug/run_performance_budget_gate \
+       --reference-path '$REFERENCE_SNAPSHOT_PATH' \
+       --candidate-path '$CANDIDATE_PATH' \
+       --report-path '$REPORT_PATH' \
+       --max-p99-regression-ratio '$MAX_P99_REGRESSION_RATIO' \
+       --coverage-floor '$COVERAGE_FLOOR'"
+  if [[ ! -f "$CANDIDATE_PATH" ]]; then
+    echo "[performance-budget-gate] missing copied candidate: $CANDIDATE_PATH" >&2
+    exit 1
+  fi
+  scp -q "$RCH_SELECTED_WORKER:$REPORT_PATH" "$REPORT_PATH"
+  if [[ ! -f "$REPORT_PATH" ]]; then
+    echo "[performance-budget-gate] missing copied report: $REPORT_PATH" >&2
+    exit 1
+  fi
+else
+  run_cargo run -p fnp-conformance --bin generate_benchmark_baseline -- \
+    --output-path "$CANDIDATE_PATH"
+  run_cargo run -p fnp-conformance --bin run_performance_budget_gate -- \
+    --reference-path "$REFERENCE_SNAPSHOT_PATH" \
+    --candidate-path "$CANDIDATE_PATH" \
+    --report-path "$REPORT_PATH" \
+    --max-p99-regression-ratio "$MAX_P99_REGRESSION_RATIO" \
+    --coverage-floor "$COVERAGE_FLOOR"
+fi
 
 echo "[performance-budget-gate] completed"
